rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract syntax and ensure all identifiers are visible in the target scope.
generic_rules: Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must use numeric expressions, and Boolean variables must use Boolean expressions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Write assumptions and guarantees using visible component ports and declared helpers only.
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for complex expressions and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for assignments.
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not encode AADL timing properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters not directly tied to visible component features
Implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair actions
